title: Goodbye Yellow Brick Road
author: Elton John
note: Many great songs. Think he's a bit overrated overall.

title: Norman Fucking Rockwell
author: Lana Del Rey
note: Four or five great songs.

title: The Marshall Mathers LP
author: Eminem
note: Some famous ones. So vulgar. The intros and outros are wild.

title: After the Gold Rush
author: Neil Young
note: Great sounding guitar. Cool lyrics. An icon.

title: Get Rich or Die Tryin'
author: 50 Cent
note: Good bangers. A bit repetitive.

title: Horses
author: Patti Smith
note: Supposed to be poetic but I didn't get it.

title: Doggystyle
author: Snoop Dogg
note: Three or four bangers. Not sure about the intros and outros.

title: Golden Hour
author: Kacey Musgraves
note: Matter of taste.

title: I Put a Spell on You
author: Nina Simone
note: Two or three good songs. Good vibe, no real bad ones.

title: The Fame Monster
author: Lady Gaga
note: Really like the first disc. Second half is okay.

title: Back in Black
author: AC/DC
note: The hits are the hits. Others are decent.

title: Listen Without Prejudice
author: George Michael
note: First three songs are excellent. Good vocalist.

title: Flower Boy
author: Tyler, the Creator
note: Who Dat Boy is a banger. A couple others good, a couple I didn't like.

title: A Seat at the Table
author: Solange
note: Not my genre. Personal preference.

title: Untrue
author: Burial
note: Great atmospheric beats, seems ahead of its time for 2007. Made me inspect my bias for needing a drop.

title: Confessions
author: Usher
note: Caught Up. Yeah.

title: Pure Heroine
author: Lorde
note: So many hits. Tennis Court, Team. Not a bad one on it.

title: Astroworld
author: Travis Scott
note: So many bangers. Sicko Mode lives up to the hype. Didn't know 21 Savage was on it.

title: Rage Against the Machine
author: Rage Against the Machine
note: Highly political and aggressive. Killing in the Name is clearly a banger.

title: Hotel California
author: Eagles
note: Life in the Fast Lane and Hotel California are excellent. Others a bit slow and country for me.

title: Body Talk
author: Robyn
note: Not enough oomph to be good techno, not enough melody to be good pop. Didn't feel it.

title: Inglourious Basterds
author: Quentin Tarantino
note: Brilliant. Dialogue is chef's kiss.

title: Our Man in Havana
author: Graham Greene
note: Dabble into fiction. Greene's main characters are always morally ambiguous and it works.

title: On Tyranny
author: Timothy Snyder
note: "Eternal Vigilance is the price of Liberty" Good historical examples. Seemed a bit ideological.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
note: Brilliant and well known.

title: Holy Cow
author: Sarah Macdonald
note: Very entertaining. A solid description of India from an Australian perspective.

title: Kitchen Confidential
author: Anthony Bourdain
note: Iconic. Makes you want to work in hospo again.

title: The Power of Culture
author: Michaela & Katharine Birbalsingh
note: Convincing.

title: Waking Up
author: Sam Harris
note: Influential and totally correct in my opinion.

title: Discover Your Inner Economist
author: Tyler Cowen
note: You'll never make another decision again without thinking economically!

title: Vagabonding
author: Rolf Potts
note: Influential and great writing. The perfect case for long-term travel.

title: We Are What We Eat
author: Alice Waters
note: A cool look into slow food. Probably overlooks the economic realities.

title: The End of Race Politics
author: Coleman Hughes
note: Thoughtful and convincing.

title: Running the Room
author: Tom Bennett
note: Canonical text on behaviour management.

title: Technopoly
author: Neil Postman
note: Brilliant and prescient. Ideas are more salient each time I read it. Look past the rambling.

title: Why We Sleep
author: Matthew Walker
note: Got me sleep-pilled.

title: Mindset
author: Carol Dweck
note: Amazing book on mindset. Perfectly informational and engaging. Mind-changing.

title: So Good They Can't Ignore You
author: Cal Newport
note: Mind-changing in the realm of career advice and work.

title: Slow Productivity
author: Cal Newport
note: Outlines a sustainable future of work. A bit too similar to his other books.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
note: Influential and original. Great actionable insights.

title: Shoe Dog
author: Phil Knight
note: Great for my 20-year-old self. Engaging story.

title: Ego Is the Enemy
author: Ryan Holiday
note: Good stories, influenced me at the time. Can't quite get around Holiday's style.

title: The Richest Man in Babylon
author: George S. Clason
note: One of the first non-fiction books I read. Sound financial advice.

title: This Is Going to Hurt
author: Adam Kay
note: So funny.

title: One Blade of Grass
author: Henry Shukman
note: A memoir about Zen and enlightenment from a normal person.
